instruments: Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 (SI 2026/102)
Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing and Transfer of Funds (Information on the Payer) Regulations 2017
FCA Policy Statement PS26/11: Crypto Regime -- Regulated Cryptoasset Activities
FCA Policy Statement PS26/12: Crypto Regime -- A Prudential Regime for Cryptoasset Firms
Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) (Amendment) Order 2023
standing_summary: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 (SI 2026/102), made by HM Treasury on 4 February 2026, creates the new FCA-regulated activities that anchor this pillar: safeguarding of qualifying cryptoassets and 'relevant specified investment cryptoassets' (including arranging for another person to safeguard them); dealing in qualifying cryptoassets as principal; dealing in qualifying cryptoassets as agent; arranging (bringing about) deals in qualifying cryptoassets, or making arrangements with a view to such transactions; and qualifying cryptoasset staking. Per the instrument's Explanatory Memorandum, full commencement -- the point at which carrying on these activities without authorisation becomes a contravention -- is 25 October 2027, matching the trading-platform activity covered in the Exchanges (VATP) pillar. The FCA published final conduct rules for all four activities on 30 June 2026 in PS26/11 (Crypto Regime: Regulated Cryptoasset Activities): for safeguarding, targeted exceptions to trust requirements, a technology-agnostic approach to private-key management, a settlement-float limit raised to 2%, and a new CASS 17 cryptoasset-custody chapter of the Handbook; for dealing and arranging, principal dealers are removed from pre-trade transparency requirements and best execution is framed around 'effective overarching arrangements' rather than transaction-by-transaction checks; for staking, firms may take client consent covering ongoing auto-staking of current and future holdings (subject to annual notification) and the FCA clarified record-keeping expectations for liquid-staking models. Prudential requirements for firms carrying on these activities were finalised the same day in PS26/12 (A Prudential Regime for Cryptoasset Firms), which places them in new COREPRU (core) and CRYPTOPRU (cryptoasset-specific) Handbook sourcebooks under a K-factor capital regime. The FCA's authorisation gateway is scheduled to open 30 September 2026 and close 28 February 2027, with a savings provision allowing firms that apply within that window to keep operating pending determination. Notably, the SI does not create a new 'advising on qualifying cryptoassets' regulated activity: per the FCA's own overview of the regime, the existing advising-on-specified-investments and managing-investments activities were not extended to qualifying cryptoassets, so advisory services on (non-security) cryptoassets stay outside the FCA's perimeter both today and after 25 October 2027 -- unless the asset advised on is itself an already-regulated specified investment (a 'relevant specified investment cryptoasset', e.g. a tokenised share or bond), in which case the pre-existing advising and custody regime already applies today, independently of this SI. None of the four new activities named above is in force today. The only cryptoasset-specific obligation currently applying to firms dealing in, arranging deals in, or safeguarding qualifying cryptoassets is registration as a 'custodian wallet provider' or 'cryptoasset exchange provider' under Regulations 8L, 9 and 14A of the Money Laundering Regulations 2017, supervised by the FCA since January 2020, plus the financial promotions regime for qualifying cryptoassets (in force since 8 October 2023, FG23-3) where the firm communicates promotions to UK consumers.

open_items: The related qualifying cryptoasset trading platform (QCATP) activity, and its PS26/11/PS26/13 conduct and Handbook-application rules, are covered in the Exchanges (VATP) pillar, not here, to avoid duplicating the same 4 Feb 2026 SI and 30 Jun 2026 policy statements across two pillars.

This pillar's name includes 'Advisory', but the FCA has not created a new 'advising on qualifying cryptoassets' regulated activity under SI 2026/102 -- non-security cryptoasset advice stays outside the FCA's perimeter even after 25 October 2027, per the FCA's own FSMA/Handbook overview page. This distinction was corroborated on that HTML page but not independently checked against the SI's own schedule text.

The PS26/11 and PS26/12 PDFs could not be parsed directly during this research (both returned corrupted/fragmented text on fetch); the safeguarding, dealing/arranging, staking and prudential detail above is corroborated via the FCA's own HTML overview page and via secondary legal-commentary summaries of the policy statements, and should be re-confirmed against the full policy-statement text directly.
The FCA's MLR registration/rejection statistics for custodian wallet providers and exchange providers belong to the AML/CFT & Enforcement pillar and are not duplicated here.

Whether firms already registered as custodian wallet providers under the MLR 2017 will receive any transitional or deemed status toward the new safeguarding/dealing/arranging/staking authorisations from 30 September 2026 was not confirmed by a primary source found during this research.
